articles.title: Energy Secretary Says Gas Prices May Stay Above $3 Until 2027
articles.summary: Secretary of Energy Chris Wright’s acknowledgment in a TV interview undercut President Trump’s earlier claim that price increases would be “short-term.”

articles.title: Muslim Southerners Face a Fresh Wave of Hateful Political Rhetoric
articles.summary: Some Muslim voters were once drawn to Republican positions on family values and individual liberty, but as Southern politicians stoke anti-Islamic sentiment, many feel threatened.

articles.title: Tired of waiting for your EV to charge up? One Chinese company has a novel solution
articles.summary: Chinese car company NIO is putting up EV battery swapping stations all around the world. NPR took a ride in one car for the experience.

articles.title: Hamas Officials Say Group Is Ready to Hand Over Some Weapons
articles.summary: Two senior Hamas officials in Gaza said the group was prepared to relinquish some automatic rifles and other arms, a concession that falls short of Israeli-U.S. demands.

articles.title: Trump Administration Demand for Ballot Info Rebuffed by Michigan
articles.summary: The Department of Justice is seeking ballots and other materials from the 2024 election. Michigan officials call it election interference.

articles.title: Eight children killed in a shooting in Louisiana, police say
articles.summary: The victims ranged in age from one to about 14 years old, said Shreveport, Louisiana police spokesperson Chris Bordelon. A total of 10 people were shot.

articles.title: They’re Young. They’re Conservative. And They’re Split Over the War.
articles.summary: On college campuses from the Northeast to the Southwest, the conflict in Iran is testing the fealty of young Republicans during the second Trump administration.

articles.title: Syrian Billionaires Needed a Favor in Washington. They Invoked the Trump Name.
articles.summary: The attempt by the Khayyats to influence foreign policy while discussions are underway about potential Trump family deals is an increasingly common feature of the president’s second term.

articles.title: Why nearly every farmer who grows these chile peppers is a woman
articles.summary: Chile peppers are a traditional part of Indian cuisine — and a key crop for women farmers. They say it's too demanding for men. "In spite of the challenges," says one, "we've found freedom."

articles.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz, Trump says
articles.summary: The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.

articles.title: Real estate investors are buying up long-term care facilities. Residents can suffer
articles.summary: Real estate investment trusts are landlords for thousands of nursing homes, assisted living facilities, and hospitals. Some select the managers and keep close watch but deny blame for bad care.

articles.title: This tariff-refund portal is about to be America's hottest website
articles.summary: Exactly two months after the Supreme Court struck down most of President Trump's tariffs, the U.S. government has set Monday as the day when some companies can begin requesting refunds.

articles.title: She no longer remembers it's her birthday. He got her a present anyway
articles.summary: A special day can be tinged with sorrow when your partner has dementia. But then he found the perfect gift.

articles.title: Are Democrats Becoming a Party of Tax Cuts?
articles.summary: As Democrats try to find a way back to power in Washington, some see tax cuts as a quick and easy way to address affordability. The wonks are freaking out.

articles.title: In Qatar, Trapped Between the U.S. and Iran, War Forced a Reckoning
articles.summary: The gas-rich Gulf nation is in a state of “strategic shock” after the war dealt a serious blow to its economy, sending ripples around the world.

articles.title: Iowa went all-in on school choice. It's hurting this city's public schools
articles.summary: With school choice programs ascendant not just in Iowa but across the U.S., Cedar Rapids offers a preview of who wins and who loses when education meets the free market.

articles.title: The Little Probe That Could: Why Voyager 1 Matters, and Why NASA Just Switched Part of It Off
articles.summary: This week, NASA announced it had shut down one of that spacecraft's remaining science instruments — not because the mission has failed, but to keep it alive a little longer.

articles.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
articles.summary: South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.

articles.title: US Coast Guard spots overturned vessel near Saipan during search for missing ship
articles.summary: The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.

articles.title: Life jacket worn by a passenger who survived the Titanic auctioned off for over $900,000
articles.summary: A life jacket worn by a passenger on the RMS Titanic has sold at auction for 670,000 pounds, which is more than $900,000.

articles.title: A mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves 6 dead before police shot and killed the gunman
articles.summary: A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.

articles.title: Photos: How overfishing in Southeast Asia is an ecological and human crisis
articles.summary: A rare look at one of the world's most critical and understudied environmental crises. Southeast Asia produces more than half of the world's fish, yet its waters are among the most depleted and contes

articles.title: Ukraine launches specialized Russian studies program to better understand the enemy
articles.summary: Ukraine launches political Russian studies program to better understand wartime enemy.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
